<!--
DOC STRUCTURE RULES
- Exactly one H1 (#) heading at the top
- All other headings must be ## or deeper
- Do NOT hard-code section numbers
- Do NOT put title in YAML header (Quarto uses first H1)
-->
# SPEC: Database–Filesystem Question Sync (v0.0.3)

This spec describes how question data is kept in sync between the **database** (source of truth for all application behavior) and the **filesystem** (mirror/backup). It covers dual-write on create/update, the optional `db_meta.yaml` file that keeps ownership and status separate from question content, and the sync algorithm when DB and FS disagree.

---

## Goals

1. **Single source of truth** — All listing, “my questions,” tests, and resolution use the **database**. The filesystem is a mirror for backup and portability.
2. **Dual-write** — On every create or update (and on approve), the app writes the same information to the DB and to the FS. Question content stays in `meta.yaml` and type-specific files; app-only metadata (ownership, status) goes in a **separate** file so the question content remains portable.
3. **Sync rules** — When reconciling FS with DB:
   - **FS-only** versions are imported into the DB (under a configured sync owner).
   - When the same version exists in both and differs, **DB wins**: the FS copy is moved to a conflicts folder, and the DB version is written to the normal FS path.
   - DB-only versions can optionally be exported to the FS (e.g. when primary storage is Supabase and an FS backup root is set).

---

## Rationale

- **Why DB as source of truth?** The app needs consistent ownership, approval status, and listing for tests and “my questions.” The database provides a single place for that.
- **Why keep a filesystem mirror?** Backup, recovery, and the ability to use question content outside the app (e.g. in another tool or after a DB failure). The FS layout (one folder per version with `meta.yaml`, prompt, options) is human-readable and portable.
- **Why separate `db_meta.yaml`?** So that “just the questions” (meta, prompt, options) stay free of app-specific baggage. You can copy or move question folders and use them elsewhere without ownership/status; the app adds `db_meta.yaml` only when it needs to sync or mirror that metadata.

---

## Data layout

### Question content (portable)

- **Location:** `{questions_root}/{logical_id}/{version}/`
- **Files:** `meta.yaml` (id, type, version, title, domain, optional tags, created_at, modified_at), `prompt.md` or `prompt.txt`, and type-specific files (e.g. `options.yaml`). Defined in QUESTION-FORMAT-0.0.1. No ownership or status in these files. For tags, see SPEC-0.0.6.

### App metadata (optional)

- **File:** `db_meta.yaml` in the same version folder.
- **Fields:** `owner_id` (uuid), `status` (`approved` | `pending`), `proposed_by` (uuid or null).
- Written and read only by this app’s dual-write and sync logic. Can be omitted or deleted when using the folder outside the app.

### Conflicts folder

- **Reserved path:** `{questions_root}/_conflicts/`
- When sync resolves a conflict (DB wins), the previous FS version is **moved** to `_conflicts/{logical_id}/{version}_{iso_timestamp}/`. That folder keeps the same structure (meta, prompt, options, and any `db_meta.yaml`). The normal path is then overwritten with the DB version.
- List and sync logic **exclude** `_conflicts` so those copies are never treated as active questions.

---

## Dual-write behavior

After every successful DB insert or update (create question, new version, upload, approve):

1. **When storage is filesystem**  
   The version folder already exists (written by the store). The app writes or updates **only** `db_meta.yaml` in that folder with `owner_id`, `status`, `proposed_by` from the DB row.

2. **When storage is Supabase**  
   If `QUESTIONS_FS_BACKUP_ROOT` is set, the app writes the **full** version to that root: all content files (meta, prompt, type-specific) plus `db_meta.yaml`. So the backup root holds a complete mirror with both content and app metadata.

Dual-write is best-effort: FS write failures do not fail the API request.

---

## Sync algorithm

Sync runs:

- **At startup:** When the server starts, if using filesystem storage, `QUESTION_SYNC_OWNER_ID` is set, and a Supabase service role key is available (`SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` or `SERVICE_ROLE_KEY`). Runs before any user logs in.
- **Explicit:** Via `POST /api/admin/sync-questions` (authenticated).

**Steps:**

1. **List DB** — All rows from `question_versions` (logical_id, version, type, title, domain, owner_id, status, proposed_by, storage_path).
2. **List FS** — All `(logical_id, version)` under the questions root that have a `meta.yaml`, **excluding** any path under `_conflicts`.
3. **FS-only** — For each FS version not in the DB: read `db_meta.yaml` if present; if it has a non-empty `owner_id`, use that for this version; otherwise use the sync owner (env `QUESTION_SYNC_OWNER_ID` or the seeded default question owner with fixed UUID). Parse content from FS, insert into DB with that `owner_id`, `status = approved`, `storage_path = {logical_id}/{version}`; then write `db_meta.yaml` to that version folder.
4. **In both** — For each version present in both DB and FS, compare metadata (type, title, domain, owner_id, status, proposed_by). If identical, skip. If different, **DB wins**: load content from the store (before moving), move the FS version folder to `_conflicts/{logical_id}/{version}_{timestamp}`, then write the DB version (content + `db_meta.yaml`) to the normal path.
5. **DB-only (optional)** — If desired, export DB-only versions to the FS backup root so the FS eventually mirrors the full DB. (Implementation may do this when backup root is set and primary is Supabase.)

When using the **default** sync owner (no `QUESTION_SYNC_OWNER_ID` set), the app uses a single seeded user (fixed UUID). Without running the seed (e.g. hosted Supabase), startup sync is skipped and the UI shows a banner with remediation steps; set `QUESTION_SYNC_OWNER_ID` to a valid user UUID to enable sync.

---

## Configuration

| Variable | Purpose |
|----------|---------|
| `QUESTIONS_ROOT` | Root for question version folders (default: `./questions`). |
| `QUESTIONS_STORAGE` | Omit or unset for filesystem; set to `supabase` for Supabase Storage. |
| `QUESTION_SYNC_OWNER_ID` | UUID of the user to assign when importing FS-only versions into the DB. Optional when using the seeded default question owner (same fixed UUID in seed and app); if set, overrides the default. Without seed (e.g. hosted Supabase), set this to a valid user UUID for startup sync. |
| `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` or `SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` | Required for startup sync (so the app can read/write `question_versions` without a user session). |
| `QUESTIONS_FS_BACKUP_ROOT` | Optional. When `QUESTIONS_STORAGE=supabase`, mirror each version to this path (content + `db_meta.yaml`). |

---

## API and listing

- **`GET /api/questions`** — Lists from the **database**: latest approved version per logical_id, shape `{ id, type, version, title, domain }`. Sync is run at server startup (see above), not on this request.
- **`POST /api/admin/sync-questions`** — Runs sync and returns `{ imported, conflictsResolved, errors }`.

All other question operations (get by id, my questions, take test) already use the DB; only sync and content loading read/write the FS.

---

## Summary

- **DB** = source of truth for listing, ownership, and tests.
- **FS** = mirror/backup; question content in `meta.yaml` + prompt + type-specific files; app metadata in optional `db_meta.yaml`.
- **Dual-write** = after each DB insert/update, write `db_meta.yaml` (and when Supabase + backup root, write full version to backup).
- **Sync** = FS-only → import to DB; in both and different → DB wins (FS to `_conflicts`, then write DB version to FS).

This keeps the question format portable while giving the app a single, consistent place (the database) for ownership and status.
